# Turtleand Build

> Engineering fundamentals, software craft, and deep technical understanding.

## Articles
- [Electricity From First Principles](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-06-26-electricity-from-first-principles/): A beginner-friendly engineering note that explains electricity through push, flow, work, and loop before introducing voltage, current, resistance, power, Ohm's law, and energy transfer.
- [Git Worktrees Are Separate Desks](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-06-25-git-worktrees-separate-desks/): A simple mental model for Git worktrees: separate folders for the same repository history, each with its own files, staging area, and HEAD.
- [EC2 Disk and Swap Resilience: Headroom Before Emergencies](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-06-21-ec2-disk-swap-resilience/): A practical runbook for expanding EC2 disk space before increasing swap, so small servers survive temporary pressure without treating swap as real RAM.
- [Dependabot Is an Update Scout](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-06-20-dependabot-update-scout/): A beginner-friendly explanation of how Dependabot watches project dependencies, opens update pull requests, and still leaves tests and human review in charge.
- [From Refactor Tools to Change Plans](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-06-19-refactor-tools-to-change-plans/): Why AI agents make the old lesson of IDE refactoring broader: inspect the system before changing it.
- [Use .git/info/exclude for Local Notes in a Shared Repo](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-06-18-git-info-exclude-local-workspace/): How Git's local exclude file keeps personal notes, AI research, scratch scripts, and temporary files out of shared repository history.
- [Spying Without Replacing with mocker.spy](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-02-12-mocker-spy-python/): How to watch real functions without faking them.
- [Dependency Injection](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-02-02-dependency-injection-python/): Understanding dependency injection through practical Flask examples and avoiding common pitfalls.
- [Testing the Decorator Pattern](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-01-04-testing-decorator/): Understand decorators as function composition and test them safely.
- [How Passkeys Rely on Private & Public Key Signing](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2026-01-01-passkeys-private-public-key/): An engineer-friendly explanation of how cryptographic signing enables passkeys to authenticate users using key ownership instead of passwords.
- [Patching the Right Thing with mocker](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2025-11-29-mocker-python/): A quick tour on why “patch where it’s used” matters.
- [Mapping Programming Conditionals to Logic Gates](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2025-11-23-conditional-equivalences/): How if/else correspond to boolean expressions, logic gates, and why clauses can be safely reordered.
- [Set up Multiple Github accounts on Mac](https://build.turtleand.com/blog/2025-11-23-configure-multiple-github-accounts-mac/): Configure multiple GitHub accounts on the same Mac using SSH keys and per-repository settings

## About
Written by Turtleand — exploring engineering fundamentals with clarity and depth.
- Portal: https://turtleand.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/turtleand
